title: Eva 04 split stuff
wikitext:
[]: Eva-04 split stuff [Spoilers]             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sun Sep 20, 2009 12:29 am Legendary wrote: They said why. They just didn't show us the Eva itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Sep 20, 2009 12:38 am Err... the TV series showed Eva-04?

[]:             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sun Sep 20, 2009 12:40 am Ornette wrote: Err... the TV series showed Eva-04?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva4Dummies » Sun Sep 20, 2009 6:36 am The explosion has something to do with the story/plot, i guess Anno is trying to show the viewers how dangerous the n2 engine is so, also it slows down Seele's plan of instrumentation, giving an excuse to extend the plot a little longer, so they did good in keeping only the explosion.

[]:             Quote Post by R-U-4-Zeruel » Sun Sep 20, 2009 11:56 am UltraBlackPilot wrote: Ornette wrote: Err... the TV series showed Eva-04?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Sep 20, 2009 12:08 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: Ornette wrote: Err... the TV series showed Eva-04?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Sep 20, 2009 12:56 pm I remember arguing with people on here months ago with people who were 100% we'd get an epic on the ground viewing of what happened to Evangelion Unit 04. Nope. Unit 04 is a plot device and really only exists visually in the much created merchandise. I bet Unit 07 from 3.0 is going to be the same thing. An Eva we never actually see but Khara/Gainax can make a mint by creating toys of it regardless. And the fans will eat it up.

[]:             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sun Sep 20, 2009 4:56 pm R-U-4-Zeruel wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Sep 20, 2009 5:10 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: why so plot hole filled?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sun Sep 20, 2009 5:12 pm Right. Like though I dont wanna see it, but Ive heard people say that the really creepy part of SAW was that you didnt really see what happened to the victims you just saw the blood. But excuse Im diverging from topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Sep 20, 2009 5:43 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: They didn't do a lot of stuff in NGE that is being done in RoE. What is your point?

[]:             Quote Post by R-U-4-Zeruel » Sun Sep 20, 2009 6:26 pm getting rid of my old post to adhere to orum rules and reg. Try finding your Eva unit 04 on those pachinko machines they have a sequance with it fighting ramiel, it has nothing to do with rebuild but has its arty style and looks pretty damn amazing

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Sep 20, 2009 8:39 pm UltraBlackPilot.Chill out.Just because you dont see Eva 04 at all in the series or Rebuild doesnt mean what happens isnt important.Dont act all high and mighty because of one thing.What happened was destined to happen.If you have a complaint,then go over to Japan and tell the people who made the series and Rebuild instead of whining.Besides,if you look hard enough you'll find a few pictures of what Eva 04 was suppose to look like. .

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Sep 21, 2009 1:45 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: R-U-4-Zeruel wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Mon Sep 21, 2009 3:19 pm I really must say, excluding the aspect of S2 Engines in Rebuild was definitely NOT a good plot move on Khara's part, Unit 04's activation seems to make less sense with the storyline now. This is also somewhat applicable to Unit 01 unless in Rebuild: 2 Cores = Godlike power only an S2 organ would give it in Neon Genesis. I suppose the Unit 04 activation could work if they tried to implement that idea, but it really just sounds kind of poorly done to me. They REALLY should have thought this out a little more.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Sep 21, 2009 3:31 pm Eva-04 isn't a Production Model in Rebuild though. It's a "Next Generation Testbed", so it's not like Eva-03, so the explosion makes plausible sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Sep 21, 2009 3:34 pm One thing remains clear however, EVA-04 was the most badass EVA unit made to date. EVA-03 was the "ultimate Eva", but -04 was it's monotoned clone with all the "proof of concept" tech thrown on... sad we never got to see any of it in action. :( As for EVA-07, I'm thinking -05 clone, though if it turns out to be the "human compliment"... God damn it...

[]:             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Mon Sep 21, 2009 10:47 pm roblucci01 wrote: I really must say, excluding the aspect of S2 Engines in Rebuild was definitely NOT a good plot move on Khara's part, Unit 04's activation seems to make less sense with the storyline now. This is also somewhat applicable to Unit 01 unless in Rebuild: 2 Cores = Godlike power only an S2 organ would give it in Neon Genesis. I suppose the Unit 04 activation could work if they tried to implement that idea, but it really just sounds kind of poorly done to me. They REALLY should have thought this out a little more.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Sep 22, 2009 8:43 am Removal of the S2 engine doesn't bother me. You'll have to view it in the entire context of the piece. Maybe Anno and Friends have so changed up what the Evangelions ultimately are (underneath the surface) that the whole S2 thing is irrelevant. As for the Massive Explosion in America, dude, these people are working with creatures that have the capability to rip holes in space and bring about the end of the world. Sometimes a poor techie is gonna cross the green wire with the red wire and cause some trouble. It doesn't bother me if some things are left unexplained. Gives me a chance to use my IMAGINATION.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Sep 22, 2009 12:36 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Sometimes a poor techie is gonna cross the green wire with the red wire and cause some trouble. It doesn't bother me if some things are left unexplained. Gives me a chance to use my IMAGINATION.
